title: Nested Conditional (Tenure Bands)
prompt: Create tenure bands: Under 1 Year, 1-5 Years, Over 5 Years based on Hire Date.
expectedBehavior: Agent builds nested `If()` statements checking tenure thresholds in order, declares Text output type.
aiResponse:
[{"id":"b526b697-63cb-452c-b2b0-28391259a70d","agentId":"0edd0f1d-d9b6-4bc5-8054-b35cd7557c8c","sessionId":"5c76aa43-6a76-49d1-b792-e7ca4d4b9a2d","role":"user","content":"Create tenure bands: Under 1 Year, 1-5 Years, Over 5 Years based on Hire Date.","timestamp":"2026-02-11T14:04:46.242Z"},{"id":"1b46202d-8f73-412b-9c50-d819f8ddd767","agentId":"0edd0f1d-d9b6-4bc5-8054-b35cd7557c8c","sessionId":"5c76aa43-6a76-49d1-b792-e7ca4d4b9a2d","role":"assistant","content":"Okay! I can create tenure bands based on hire date. Here's the expression:

```

This expression will produce a **Text** output.

Suggested column name: **Tenure Band**

{{SUGGESTED_ACTIONS:Use this expression|Validate with sample data|Explain this expression}}","timestamp":"2026-02-11T14:04:47.636Z"}]

title: Excel-Style IF
expectedBehavior: Agent explains that Excel-style syntax is not supported, then provides the equivalent expression using `If()`, `Multiply()`, `Divide()`, and `Value()`.
aiResponse:
[{"id":"b5975f05-6e80-4680-9eba-621c320b4c57","agentId":"0edd0f1d-d9b6-4bc5-8054-b35cd7557c8c","sessionId":"b3f006c0-2439-4424-986f-deb4fa55c262","role":"user","content":"=IF(Pay Type='Hourly', Hourly Pay*40, Annual Salary/52)","timestamp":"2026-02-11T14:05:16.855Z"},{"id":"84742c8d-8dab-4c0d-9d0e-48824c8438d6","agentId":"0edd0f1d-d9b6-4bc5-8054-b35cd7557c8c","sessionId":"b3f006c0-2439-4424-986f-deb4fa55c262","role":"assistant","content":"That looks like an Excel-style formula. I can convert it into the correct format for you.

Here's the expression in the required function-call syntax:

```
If(Eq(PayType, "Hourly"), Multiply(Value(HourlyPay), 40), Divide(Value(AnnualSalary), 52))
```

Note that I've also added `Value()` around `HourlyPay` and `AnnualSalary`. This is needed because, by default, columns are treated as text. To perform math on them, you must explicitly convert them to numbers using the `Value()` function.

This expression will produce a **Numeric** output.

Suggested column name: **Calculated Pay**

{{SUGGESTED_ACTIONS:Use this expression|Validate with sample data|Explain this expression}}","timestamp":"2026-02-11T14:05:18.599Z"}]

title: Excel-Style SUM
expectedBehavior: Agent corrects to `Add(Value(Scheduled EE Amount), Value(Scheduled ER Amount))`.
aiResponse:
[{"id":"a93c44aa-5cd2-4899-aeb0-f1d707b89e48","agentId":"0edd0f1d-d9b6-4bc5-8054-b35cd7557c8c","sessionId":"2f5580a1-f308-42a0-8136-2d6e5929f6b4","role":"user","content":"=SUM(Scheduled EE Amount, Scheduled ER Amount)","timestamp":"2026-02-11T14:05:20.607Z"},{"id":"be3acfce-640d-4d65-bdb0-b401fcb7d142","agentId":"0edd0f1d-d9b6-4bc5-8054-b35cd7557c8c","sessionId":"2f5580a1-f308-42a0-8136-2d6e5929f6b4","role":"assistant","content":"That looks like an Excel-style formula. To create a calculated column, you'll need to use function-call syntax. The equivalent expression would be `Add(ScheduledEEAmount, ScheduledERAmount)`. Also, because "Scheduled EE Amount" and "Scheduled ER Amount" are text columns, you'll need to convert them to numbers using the `Value()` function.

Here's the expression:
```
Add(Value(ScheduledEEAmount), Value(ScheduledERAmount))
```
This expression will produce a **Numeric** output.

Suggested column name: **Total Scheduled Amount**
{{SUGGESTED_ACTIONS:Use this expression|Validate with sample data|Explain this expression}}","timestamp":"2026-02-11T14:05:22.054Z"}]
